entities.name: LITTLE MILLET
entities.description: A nutritious and climate-resilient crop of the Poaceae family originating from India, cultivated in marginal soils.

entities.name: POACEAE
entities.description: The botanical family to which little millet belongs.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The region of origin for little millet.

entities.name: MARGINAL SOILS
entities.description: Low-fertility soils where little millet is predominantly grown.

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Environmental shifts to which little millet shows resilience.

entities.name: FOOD AND NUTRITION SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food.

entities.name: NANA
entities.description: One of the two cultivated races of little millet.

entities.name: ROBUSTA
entities.description: One of the two cultivated races of little millet.

entities.name: GENE BANKS
entities.description: Repositories that conserve genetic diversity of crop species.

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of enhancing the genetic traits of a crop for better performance.

entities.name: PRIMARY SELECTION
entities.description: A method of developing improved genotypes from landraces.

entities.name: LANDRACES
entities.description: Local varieties of crops that have developed over time in a specific location.

entities.name: HYBRIDIZATION TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Methods used for the development and release of new crop varieties.

entities.name: GENOMIC TOOLS
entities.description: Advanced biological tools used to accelerate the development of improved cultivars.

entities.name: HIGH YIELD
entities.description: The capacity of a crop to produce a large amount of harvestable product.

entities.name: NUTRIENT DENSITY
entities.description: The concentration of essential nutrients within the crop.

entities.name: STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand adverse environmental conditions.

entities.name: BIOTIC AND ABIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Environmental pressures including biological pests/diseases and non-living factors.

entities.name: BREEDING TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Targeted methods used for the improvement and wider adoption of crops.

entities.name: CROPPING SYSTEM
entities.description: The pattern of crops and management practices used on a piece of land.

entities.name: VALUE ADDITION
entities.description: The process of increasing the economic value of a primary agricultural product.
